verses:
svataḥ⋅siddhi rati tā̐’re sambandhādi⋅sahākāre s
samartha kariyā rākhe sadā
kṛṣṇa⋅sevā vinā tā̐’ra udyama nāhika āra
svīya sukha ceṣṭa nāhi kadā

ei rati prauḍha haya mahā⋅bhāva dāsa pāya
jāra tulya prāpti āra nāi
sarvādbhuta camatkāra sambhogecchā e prakāra
varṇibāre vākya nāhi pāi
en_translation:
The self-perfected love for the Lord, combined with a realization of one's relationship with Him, always keeps a person spiritually capable. Without service to Lord Kṛṣṇa, there is no other endeavor for such a person, and they never make any attempt for their own happiness.

This mature love eventually leads one to become a servant of the highest spiritual emotions. There is no other attainment equal to this. This kind of most wonderful and astonishing desire for divine enjoyment—I find no words to describe it.
